Noah Patel: Who's the current contract signer? Are they asking for credit or term change?

Liam O'Neill: Finance: need legal name, VAT, remit address. For seat drop we'll issue prorated credit. PO preferred but CC acceptable to speed up.

Ava Chen: Effective date requested 2026-06-15. They've sent a delegation pdf to procurement@redwood.com.

Marta (CS Manager): Can procurement email + doc suffice? @Priya confirm.

Priya Rao: Yes — procurement email from company domain + delegation doc acceptable. I'll draft one-page addendum now.

Noah Patel: I'll confirm delegated signer's name. If we can get CC we can close faster before PO.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xdef456: Sent to elena.t@helios.com for signature (1/3). Status: sent.

Ava Chen: Link to delegation doc: https://files.redwood/helios/delegation-0612.pdf

Noah Patel: Procurement confirms Elena Torres (Head of Procurement) as delegated signer. Billing requested to be "Helios EU BV".

Liam O'Neill: Please provide VAT: BE987654321 and remit address. We'll update Zuora and issue corrected invoice post-signature.

Priya Rao: Draft addendum uploaded: https://drive.redwood/helios/addendum-2026-06-15.docx. Text includes: "Seat reduction effective 2026-06-15; prorated credit to next invoice; billing entity changed to Helios EU BV; no other contract terms changed." Review pls.

Ava Chen: Pricing same per-seat?

Noah Patel: Confirmed: same per-seat rate.

Liam O'Neill: We'll calculate prorate by days in billing period (UTC). Please confirm effective date inclusive.

Priya Rao: Inserted account HEL-12345 and updated billing entity. Routing for signature now.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xdef456: Completed by Elena Torres. Status: completed.

Marta (CS Manager): Great. @Priya upload signed addendum to customer folder and send to billing.

Priya Rao: Uploaded and stamped 2026-06-10 counter-sign date. Forwarding to accounting.

Ava Chen: Gainsight updated, renewal risk down. Offering CC fallback if PO delays.

Noah Patel: Will log PO when received and confirm closure.

Liam O'Neill: Billing summary:

Account: HEL-12345
Old seats: 112
New seats: 100
Effective: 2026-06-15
Prorate: days-in-period calculation, credit to next invoice
Billing entity: Helios EU BV (VAT on file)
```

Ava Chen: Closing thread — resolved in CS tracker. Thanks everyone :white_check_mark:

title: #customer-success: 3987654321
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads up — Midterm renewal ask from Helios Labs. They want to reduce 15 seats mid-contract and move billing to their EU entity. Procurement is requesting a delegated signer on our Docusign envelope. Need quick alignment on legal/finance/exec path — any red flags?

Noah Patel: Thanks. Who's the current signer on the contract? Also are they asking for prorate credit or a term adjustment?

Priya Rao: Legal: we can allow a delegated signer if we receive a written delegation (procurement email + org chart). EU entity swap requires tax ID & PO or invoice remit details. No T&C material changes ok. We'll need a short addendum referencing the account id.

Liam O'Neill: Finance: we'll need the EU billing entity legal name, VAT/tax id, and remit address. For 15-seat reduction we will issue a prorated credit effective the reduction date. If they insist on PO that will slow closure.

Ava Chen: They want effective 2026-05-01. Procurement prefers PO but can pay by CC to speed things. They've already sent a delegation PDF to procurement@redwood.com.

Marta (CS Manager): Can we accept the delegation letter via procurement email thread? If yes, let's route the envelope to the delegated signer. @Priya confirm email + doc is sufficient.

Priya Rao: Email + procurement header from company domain + delegation letter is acceptable. We still need a short signed addendum stating seat change and billing entity swap. I'll draft it now.

Noah Patel: I'll confirm the delegated signer's full name and authority. If we need an addendum, can we do a one-pager addendum with line items? I can push for CC to close before PO lands.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xabc123: Sent to noah.p@helios.com for signature (1/3). Status: sent.

Ava Chen: Copy of delegation letter (redacted): https://files.redwood/helios/delegation-2026-04.pdf

Noah Patel: Procurement replied — delegated signer is Elena Torres (Title: Head of Procurement). They request invoice billed to "Helios EU BV". Attached vendor confirmation.

Liam O'Neill: OK. For Helios EU BV we need VAT: BE123456789 (example), and remit address. We'll update billing system (Zuora) and issue a corrected invoice after signature.

Priya Rao: Draft addendum uploaded: https://drive.redwood/helios/addendum-seat-reduction-2026.docx. Key text: "Seat reduction effective 2026-05-01; prorated credit applied to next invoice; billing entity changed to Helios EU BV; no other contract terms altered." Please review quickly.

Ava Chen: Quick QA — pricing unchanged? They want same per-seat rate, right?

Noah Patel: Confirmed: same per-seat price, just fewer seats. If legal ok with addendum, we can route for signature.

Liam O'Neill: Finance note: we'll calculate prorate by days in the billing period. Please confirm effective date is inclusive. Also send exact timezone if they care (we'll use UTC date-only).

Priya Rao: Signed addendum template ready. I'll insert account id HEL-12345 and update the billing entity line.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xabc123: Signed by Elena Torres (delegated signer). Status: completed.

Marta (CS Manager): Nice work. @Priya can you upload the final signed addendum to the customer folder and forward to accounting for invoice update?

Priya Rao: On it — uploading now and will stamp counter-signed date.

Ava Chen: Helios confirmed onboarding checklist updated and seat counts adjusted. Updating Gainsight and renewal forecast now — risk decreases. Thanks everyone :tada:

Noah Patel: I'll log the PO number when it comes through and notify finance. Closing loop.

Liam O'Neill: Billing snippet for records:
```
Account: HEL-12345
Old seats: 200
New seats: 185
Effective: 2026-05-01
Prorate: calculated by days, credit applied to next invoice
Billing entity: Helios EU BV (VAT on file)
```

Ava Chen: Closing thread — marking as resolved in CS tracker. If PO stalls, we'll offer temporary CC. :white_check_mark:

title: #customer-success: 3288881234
preview:
Ethan Park (Orbit): Hey CS, onboarding hiccup. After ~75s our service started seeing a 403 followed by a burst of 429s. Logs say 'token_invalid' then 'quota_exceeded: per_key'. Demo in 48h, can we triage?
Priya Khatri: Thanks Ethan. Which SDK/version and are you routing through a proxy or direct to API?
Ethan Park (Orbit): Node SDK v2.2.0 behind our API gateway. We mint short tokens TTL=120s, rotate 25s before expiry, and keep prior token for 50s so streams don't drop.
Jordan Ruiz: Keeping the previous token for 50s is likely the culprit if your gateway multiplexes connections. Two distinct tokens hitting auth in quick succession can count twice against the per-key quota.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Repro log snippet: 
# t=0 -> 200 OK
# t=80 token swap -> 403 {"error":"token_invalid"}
# immediate retries -> 429 {"error":"quota_exceeded","quota":"per_key"}
Jordan Ruiz: Two things to try: 1) reduce old-token retention to <=5s for a quick validation, 2) increase retry backoff to 150-250ms to avoid tight retry bursts.
Priya Khatri: We can also temporarily relax per-key window for Orbit while you validate. If the quick test passes we'll update onboarding docs to call out header-buffering behavior behind multiplexing proxies.
CS Bot: Ticket created: CS-9123  labels: onboarding, auth, rate-quota. Temp relax enabled until 3288884999.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Will try old-token keep=3s + backoff=200ms now, will report back in ~15m. Thanks.
Jordan Ruiz: If you use long streaming, prefer using the same token for stream lifetime and only rotate on new connections to avoid mid-stream swaps.
Priya Khatri: Follow-up: if the test is green we'll push a short playbook entry and a reproduce script for customers.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Quick test success  10m no 403/429 under ramp. Demo looks safe for now. Will confirm after wider ramp.
Priya Khatri: Great. Action items: Ethan to continue ramp and confirm, CS to add playbook note, eng to attach logs to CS-9123 and close when stable. Thanks all. :tada:

title: #customer-success: 3265432100
preview:
incident-bot: ALERT: High error rate detected on /v1/generate for dedicated-cluster-5. Error rate spikes started ~2026-03-12T02:28Z. Pager on-call: @Samir. Automated mitigation: routing fallback applied to tier-2 models.
Samir Patel: Ack, joining bridge now. setting priority=high. will post brief status page copy for Maya to push.
Maya Lopez: thanks Samir — CS team needs: 1) short status-page blurb, 2) customer email draft (single para + short timeline), 3) confirmation on data exposure.
Samir Patel: quick update: rolled back last routing policy change (deploy id: route-2026-03-12-02). Fallback to model-variant b1 reduced errors from ~18% -> ~1.2% in 6m. Still seeing intermittent 429s for few tenants on cluster-5. Investigating root cause (suspect compile/kernel selection mismatch after that route change).
Ben Carter: saw surge in compile backlog on g4-pool around 02:26Z. log snippet: ```ERROR compile_queue_timeout model=redwood-opus-v2 shard=3 duration_ms=5020``` Backpressure propagated and caused generator to return 503/429.
Samir Patel: proposed status page blurb below — Maya feel free to shorten. Also adding a CS email draft after that.
Samir Patel: STATUS PAGE (proposed): 
- "Incident: Increased error rates for generation requests to dedicated-cluster-5. Mitigation: traffic routed to fallback model variants; errors substantially reduced. Impact: some requests may have experienced 5xx/429 responses between 02:25–02:40 UTC. Current status: degraded (mitigation in place)."
Samir Patel: CUSTOMER EMAIL DRAFT (proposed): 
"Hi Eli — we experienced increased errors affecting generation requests on your dedicated cluster for a ~15 minute window today (2026-03-12 02:25–02:40 UTC). We routed traffic to fallback model variants which reduced failed calls and restored normal service. We do not believe any customer data was exposed. We will share a full timeline, root cause analysis, and proposed preventative actions within 48 hours. Apologies for the disruption — Jordan will follow up with credit details and next steps.
— Samir & Redwood Support"
Jordan (CSM): draft looks good. For BrightApps can we state tentative credit: 10% of the invoice for the affected hour(s) or equivalent $ credit? Also add line: "If you'd like, we can schedule a 30-min call to walk the timeline and mitigation."
Maya Lopez: also add a one-line exec summary at top: "Service briefly degraded for a subset of dedicated customers; mitigation applied."
Eli (BrightApps): thanks — appreciate the quick mitigation. Two Qs: 1) timeline please, 2) was any user data or logs sent to 3rd-party?
Samir Patel: timeline (preliminary):
- 02:24: change route-2026-03-12-02 deployed to routing plane
- 02:25: compile backlog spike observed on g4-pool -> increased compile latency
- 02:26: first 5xx/429 observed for some tenants on cluster-5
- 02:31: automated routing fallback triggered, some traffic shifted to b1 variants
- 02:34: manual rollback of route-2026-03-12-02 completed (traffic restored to previous stable route)
- 02:40: error rate returned to baseline (~0.1%)
Samir Patel: on data exposure: we ran quick grep and audit of egress paths and 3rd-party integrations for affected instances — no evidence of data leaving Redwood boundaries. command used: `rg "upload_to_s3|external_callback" /var/logs/cluster-5 -n --no-ignore-vcs` returned no matches for the affected timeframe.
Ben Carter: remediation actions for post-incident roadmap: 1) add circuit-breaker on routing changes to prevent wide fanout of unvalidated rules, 2) increase compile workers for g4-pool during deploy windows, 3) add tighter compile-time SLO and early detection alerts.
Samir Patel: agreed. I'll capture these in the RCA doc. Action items:
- Samir: produce full RCA draft + prevention plan within 48h (assigned)
- Ben: run capacity check and propose autoscale tweak for g4-pool by EOD + add test for compile backlog alerts
- Jordan: prepare customer credit proposal for BrightApps (suggest 10% credit for impacted minute-window) and schedule follow-up call if Eli wants
- Maya: update status page with approved short blurb and link to follow-up RCA when published
Jordan (CSM): Eli — we propose a 10% credit for the impacted hour prorated across your usage that window. If that sounds OK we'll process it and include in the next billing cycle. Want to set a 30-min call Wednesday 10:00 UTC?
Eli (BrightApps): 30-min Wed 10:00 UTC works. Credit proposal sounds fair. Also please confirm whether the fallback model affected tokenization/outputs for ongoing prompts — any risk of silent semantic changes?
Samir Patel: fallback model b1 is a compatible variant with same tokenizer; we ran spot checks on customer prompts and found equivalent outputs within acceptable variance. No silent truncation or PII leaks discovered.
Jordan (CSM): noted — scheduling call and will send calendar invite. We'll send a short customer-facing timeline + credit confirmation after Samir posts the RCA draft.
Maya Lopez: thanks team. Closing notes: keep thread for RCA link + final status page URL. :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: will update thread with RCA link and final mitigation checklist when ready.
Jordan (CSM): thanks everyone.

title: #customer-success: 3135792468
preview:
Elena Ward: Quick batch update — pulled 20 in-product survey rows (NPS followups), 14 voice snippets from onboarding, and 6 support threads. Aim: surface top 8 distinct customer asks (atomic), tag product/enablement/infra, and mark any "renewal_cohort" customers for rapid escalation. Need a tidy CSV + 1-slide summary for PM sync tomorrow.

Marcus Lin: I can run the first pass. Plan: merge telemetry (event_id) + ASR transcripts + support excerpts, run sentiment + NLU extraction, then human-validate low-confidence items. Will output one row per atomic ask.

- churn_alert = renewal_cohort ? 1 : 0 (and escalate if signal_score >=7 || churn_alert==1)
Also add 'evidence_refs' column with event_ids/clip_ids/ticket_ids.

Jorge Alvarez: CSV header suggestion (minimal):
```
customer,source,ref_id,ask,category,signal_score,churn_alert,sentiment,enablement_ok,evidence_refs,notes
```

Ava Chen: Excellent. If nothing unexpected, we'll ship the packet at 18:00 PT. I'll own the customer note and call deck.

Jake Morales: Pre-warm rollout scheduled for next week, I'll include the rollout plan in the bundle.

Ava Chen: Thanks team — big win if we can close this before the renewal review.

title: #customer-success: 2765432100
preview:
Ava Chen: FYI I pushed a small script to sync the onboarding asset checklist across playbooks. Noticed a handful of accounts missing the `welcome_call` step in HubSpot -> playbook mapping, which blocks our automated enablement emails. Rolling it out as a dry-run in staging tonight. Ping me if this would interfere with any ongoing backfills :wave:

Tom Ruiz: Thanks Ava — where's the repo/PR? want to skim before staging run. Also curious if this touches healthscore fields or just playbook metadata.

Ava Chen: PR here: https://github.com/redwood-inference/cs-ops/PR/482 (branch: cs/onboard-check-sync). It's additive — creates `onboard_assets_missing` boolean and appends missing steps to the playbook metadata. Does NOT change the computed healthscore.

Maya Patel: Phew ok. If it's only metadata we can still have playbooks pick it up for the next run. Can we do a backfill for all accounts created in the last 90 days after the staging verification? Some churned trials are impacted.

Ava Chen: Yep plan is: 1) run on staging with `--dry-run` to produce a CSV; 2) review sample of 100 accounts; 3) if looks good, run a one-off backfill in prod over 90d window. Backfill command: `python tools/sync_onboard_assets.py --window 90 --apply`

Jonah Klein: Can you paste the SQL you used to detect missing steps? want to ensure no joins that explode.

Ava Chen: SQL used to find gaps — lightweight:
```sql
SELECT a.account_id, p.playbook_id, s.step_key
FROM accounts a
LEFT JOIN playbook_steps p ON p.playbook_id = a.playbook_id
LEFT JOIN account_steps s ON s.account_id = a.account_id AND s.step_key = p.step_key
WHERE p.required = true AND s.step_key IS NULL
LIMIT 100;
```
Should be fine for incremental runs; we batch per playbook to keep queries small.

Tom Ruiz: Cool. Two asks: 1) add a `dry_run_sample` flag so we can easily rerun with a deterministic seed; 2) emit a small audit event for each change so CS can review in the console later.

Ava Chen: Good calls — added `--seed` and I emit `cs.onboard.asset_sync` events to `cs-audit` topic. Events include account_id, before_steps, after_steps, actor.

Maya Patel: About risk scoring — can we have an opt-in toggle so accounts flagged as "missing_welcome_call" don't change renewal risk until cs-review? We don't want false positives in churn alerts.

Ava Chen: Yes, default is opt-out (no risk changes). I added an optional `apply_risk_tag=true` flag for the backfill, but it's off by default. We'll coordinate with risk team before turning it on.

Jonah Klein: Sounds good. I'll schedule a staging dry-run tomorrow 09:30 PT and invite the risk / enablement folks to review the sample CSV. Please push any small fixes today so I can include them.

CS Ops Bot: deploybot: Rolling deployment for `cs/onboard-check-sync` to staging started by Ava Chen — build #842 queued. :rocket:

Ava Chen: Thanks — I'll post the dry-run CSV to #customer-success when complete and attach the sampled accounts. If anyone wants a pre-review of the CSV format DM me. :memo:

Maya Perez: FYI — LyraHealth submitted their vendor security survey and flagged two items: telemetry collection and BYOK. I uploaded their PDF: https://files.redwood.ai/lyra-survey_v1.pdf

Tom Huang: I'll take BYOK. Can someone paste the exact question wording so I can prepare a concise answer?
Ava Chen: Dropping the excerpts below.
Maya Perez: Q34 Telemetry: "Describe what runtime telemetry is collected and if customer data is included."
Q58 Keys: "Can customers bring their own keys to encrypt embeddings in Dedicated/Private deployments?"
Tom Huang: For Q58: short answer — Private supports customer-managed KMS/HSM integrations (BYOK). Dedicated uses platform-managed keys by default; enterprise BYOK add-on available. Suggest a 2-line answer + link to docs: https://docs.redwood.ai/security/kms

- Telemetry: We collect operational metrics (latency, error rates, model usage) to maintain SLOs; content or PII are not included in telemetry. Customers can disable telemetry on Private/Dedicated with an ops flag: `telemetry.collection=false`.
- BYOK: Supported for Private via KMS/HSM integrations; Dedicated customers can request BYOK through our enterprise pack — see docs and sample contract clause.
Rachel Legal: Add a brief sentence referencing our SOC 2 coverage and that BYOK will include audit log access and key-management responsibilities in the contract. I'll prepare the clause.
Maya Perez: Thanks. Ava, can you send this back to Lyra with Rachel cc'd? I'll include the PDF.
Ava Chen: Sending now to LyraHealth portal and copying legal. Will update thread when they reply. :white_check_mark:

- fallback model uploaded: yes
- alerting: latency and error-5xx set to pg 1

title: #customer-success: 2072103456
preview:
Priya Rao: Morning — quick heads-up on the Jade account dedicated pool. Noticed a saturation spike overnight, sustained around ~03:10 UTC. High-level: GPU hours up ~42% WoW, queue depth peaked at 32, spend is trending higher. Can ops/eng confirm root cause and whether this will affect the renewal convo later this week? Attaching the alert logs below.

ops-bot: ```ALERT: pool=dedicated-jade saturation=98% at 2026-03-20T03:10:22Z
queue_depth=32
95p_latency_ms=290
error_rate_pct=0.4
top_consumer=jade-analytics-job-9f2b```

Luca Moretti: On it — found a long-running batch (jade-analytics-job-9f2b) that kicked off at ~02:40 UTC and kept grabbing slots. It wasn't configured as low-priority so it displaced inference traffic. I throttled replicas and set `priorityClass=low` for the remaining runs. Current pool status: saturation ~63%, queue depth 3.
24h GPU hours: 72h (baseline ~50–52h). Estimated incremental spend ~ $820/day. Snippet from the pool metrics:```pool:deds-jade saturation:63%
queue_depth:3
gpu_hours_24h:72
cost_delta_per_day:820```

Ethan Park: Did we see customer-facing latency impact? Jade has a renewal call Friday — we need numbers for the deck.

Luca Moretti: Yes — 95p for `/jade/api/generate` rose from ~210ms -> ~290ms during the peak window. Error rate for that route nudged up ~0.2 percentage points. We pushed a portion of traffic to a smaller model (`redwood-small-v2`) as an emergency fallback which prevented 95p going above ~400ms.

Zoe Chen: Noted. For the renewal I'd recommend: 1) offer a one-time credit (proposal: 20% of this month's dedicated overage) to smooth the conversation; 2) run an optimization workshop with Eng/runtime to tune batch scheduling and autoscale; 3) add preemptible scheduling for heavy analytics jobs so they don't compete with low-latency inference. Also capture feedback: they said the scheduling docs were a bit sparse. :eyes: :calendar:

Priya Rao: Action items — Luca: file a PR to enforce `priorityClass=low` for `jade-analytics` by EOD; Ethan: prepare a 1-pager with latency impact and mitigations for the renewal deck; Zoe: draft the credit proposal and propose two workshop slots (Thu AM or Mon next week). I'll loop in the AM success lead and copy @eng-runtime for the preemptible scheduling ask.

ops-bot: ```STATUS: mitigation applied, saturation down to 63%, queue depth down to 3. Monitor window 1h.```

Priya Rao: Thanks team — keeping this thread as the single source for the renewal notes. If anyone sees a re-spike tag this thread and DM me.

title: #customer-success: 1998401234
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick weekly sync for Acme — noticed a model-mix shift after Friday's rollout. High-level: usage +23% W/W, requests 126k -> 155k, avg spend +9%. Fallbacks spiked and latency hit a 95p of ~780ms at peak. Posting details and next steps below.

Diego Martinez: When exactly was the rollout and what traffic split?

Ava Chen: Fri 11:14 UTC start (canary 20%), reached 100% by 11:40 UTC. Fallback surge started ~12:10 UTC and coincided with a short customer traffic burst.

deploy-bot: Deployment event — redwood-open-v2 rollout -> 100% at 2026-03-19T11:40:12Z :rocket:

Kai Patel: Pulled the telemetry. Key numbers: fallback rate baseline 0.9% -> peak 4.1% (15:12 minute). 95th latency for text-gen increased from 420ms -> 780ms during the spike. Error types: kvcache-miss, attention OOM.

Diego Martinez: Can you paste example error traces / logs so infra can triage quicker?

Ava Chen: copy of a couple lines from logs: `ERROR: kvcache-miss key=usr:acme:session:49f2` and `FATAL: attention_kernel OOM at step=23 model=redwood-open-v2 shards=2`. Also seeing a few `429 fallback` counts.

Olivia Park: (Acme PM) we shipped the bulk-chat feature Friday morning — sessions now include 900–1,800 token histories often. We expected more token consumption but not the degradation. Can you advise if there's a quick mitigation? :grimacing:

Diego Martinez: Short-term options: route long-context sessions to high-memory pool, reduce `max_tokens` for server-side completions, or toggle a smaller quantized variant. Proposed temporary routing rule: 
```
route-rule: if avg_tokens > 512 -> redwood-compat-highmem
else -> redwood-open-v2
```
This should reduce OOMs while we tune caching.

Kai Patel: Cost impact estimate for Friday peak: compute +9% (~$1.7k extra/day @ current unit rates). If we enable prefix/KV caching for ~30% of sessions we estimate fallback down ~1.2pp and cost reduction ~4%. I'll share the numbers on the dashboard.

Ava Chen: Action items I suggest: 1) enable routing rule (Diego) 2) infra to bump highmem pool temporarily (SRE) 3) enable prefix cache for Acme (Kai) 4) customer enablement to nudge prompt trimming where possible (Olivia). I'll coordinate the call and follow-ups.

Diego Martinez: I'll open a Dedicated ticket for pool adjustment and add a canary rollback plan in case the fix regresses latency. :thumbsup:

Olivia Park: Thanks — can we do a 30m call with Acme PMs tomorrow morning PT? They are most available 9am PT.

Ava Chen: Scheduled tentative sync: 2026-03-20 16:00 UTC (9am PT). I'll send the invite and include Diego + Kai.

Kai Patel: Working on a compact dashboard with the spike graphs and token distribution, will drop link here in ~30m.

Ava Chen: Great — please add the estimated cost delta and fallback trend. I'll update this thread after the call with outcomes. Thanks everyone. :pray:

Renewal risk: medium — concerned about cost predictability. Action: propose quota smoothing + adaptive routing; scheduling cost review with customer on Thu. Dashboard: https://console.redwood/in/orgs/cobalt/usage

Ava Chen: I pushed a temporary route rule to fallback earlier to rwd-small-v2 to avoid 429 cascades. Also added a conservative batching policy for Cobalt (max_batch_ms=30) to reduce tail latencies. Can someone drop a short traffic histogram so I can iterate parameters? :white_check_mark:

median request size: 650 tokens

```

```
route_policy:
  max_batch_ms: 30
  prefer_cache: true

```

preview:
Ava Chen: Quick capture from HelixCardio onboarding retro earlier today — writing up raw asks so we don't lose anything. TL;DR customer is happy with latency but running into visibility + control gaps.

Ava Chen: Raw asks (verbatim / summarized):
- "per-route cost breakdown per model" — they want to see which route is burning budget.
- "granular RBAC for routes and models" — legal wants only certain teams to call prod models.
- "retry/debug logs on streaming failures" — hard to triage intermittent disconnects.
- "embedding drift detection" — embeddings for search are degrading after model swap.
- "model fallback tunables" — want to control when we fallback to cheaper variants.
- "onboarding docs: quickstart for dedicated customers" — missing checklist for infra/network steps.

Miguel Torres: Nice summary. Can we prioritize 1-2 for Q2? Renewal convo next week, want a list to promise if we surface as committed work. :eyes:

Jordan Patel: Suggest we do an impact x effort quick matrix. My initial read: cost breakdown = high impact / med effort; RBAC = high impact / high effort; retry logs = med impact / low effort; embedding drift = high impact / med/high effort; fallback tunables = high impact / low-med effort; docs = low effort / med impact.

Priya Singh: Adding direct customer quotes (for context):
- "We can't justify volume until we can show where the spend goes."
- "Our security team will not accept broad keys for model calls."

Liam O'Riley: From eng POV: retry/debug logs and fallback tunables look like the fastest wins. Retry logs -> add structured events + request_id propagation, ~2 sprints. Fallback tunables -> config + route-level policy, ~1 sprint. Per-route cost needs backend work + ui, ~3-4 sprints.

Jordan Patel: OK. Proposal: mark 'per-route cost' and 'RBAC' as required for renewal (if we can't commit, we escalate). But target delivery: fallback tunables + retry logs as Q2 quickships. Can someone own triage + ticketing?

Ava Chen: I'll own the triage for CS side — will convert customer notes into acceptance criteria and hand to Jordan for PM prioritization.

Jordan Patel: I'll create tickets and add labels. Draft tickets being created: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RED-4512 (fallback-tunables), https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RED-4513 (retry-logs). Will add priority and link to this thread.

cs-bot: Action logged: "create:triage_note" by Ava Chen on 1963001123

Miguel Torres: Please add an "renewal_risk" flag on the per-route-cost item — we need that surfaced in the renewal tracker.

Ava Chen: Done. Updated triage note with renewal_risk=high and customer severity examples.

Jordan Patel: Quick checklist I'll add to each ticket (copy/paste):
```
- customer_impact: <quote>
- acceptance_criteria: <what we will demo>
- owner: <name>
- estimate: <sprint points>
- renewal_risk_flag: <high/med/low>
```

Priya Singh: Also adding: copy of onboarding notes and the customer's recommended rollout plan (they prefer canary on dedicated first). I'll drop the doc in the CS folder.

Liam O'Riley: If we go with canary for fallback tunables, we should add metrics to alert on quality regressions. Adding to ticket.

Ava Chen: Thanks team. Next steps: Jordan to add tickets + labels; Liam to add engineering estimates; Ava to push triage note and schedule a 30m sync with HelixCardio if Jordan flags we can commit.

Miguel Torres: :thumbsup: — please ping me once tickets are live; I'm on the renewal call Thurs.

Jordan Patel: Will do.

Ava Chen: Ending thread, will update when docs/tickets exist.

title: #customer-success: 1952103030
preview:
Nadia Ahmed: Heads up: HarborFlow (mid-market, renewal in 5d) flagged at-risk. Low adoption of scheduled jobs, reported spikes in token cost after a recent feature toggle. They mentioned timeouts on some batch exports. Need a concise save play we can run before renewal.
Ethan Park: Any telemetry links? Is it a new integration or change in usage patterns?
Riley Morgan: Quick look: new webhook consumer enabled last week; requests are long (avg 12k tokens) and some retries loop. Also their feature toggle accidentally set verbose logging -> extra tokens.
ops-bot: Alert: HarborFlow export retries detected, duplicate rate 6k/hr. See incident HF-2211.
Samir Desai: Two parallel tracks: immediate mitigation (rate-limit + toggle revert), and a short commercial/enablement offer for reassurance. Recommend 10-day trial credits + engineer support.
Jules Kim: I'll draft a short customer message and an enablement checklist. We should offer a scoped temporary SLA on export route if they accept remediation.
Ethan Park: Technical fixes I can push now: 1) revert verbose logging setting, 2) apply a temporary per-route throttle (300 r/s), 3) enable token cap for export endpoint (max_tokens=4000). All reversible.
Riley Morgan: I'll prepare a preload for common prefixes and a quick script to dedupe webhook retries. Also will surface p95 latency before/after.
Nadia Ahmed: Commercial: propose 2-week 20% credit + two 1-hour enablement sessions. If they commit to a 3-month reserved plan within 30d, convert credits to prepaid. Also include opt to keep one critical route on reserved capacity at current price.
Samir Desai: If they push to downgrade, use a graded downgrade: reduce non-customer-facing exports to best-effort while keeping storefront and billing routes on standard SLA. Capture that in a downgrade matrix.
Jules Kim: Draft message:
``
Hi HarborFlow team,
We saw increased export sizes and some retry loops after your recent toggle. Immediate steps we propose: (1) 30-min triage call, (2) revert verbose logging and apply temporary throttle, (3) 2-week cost credit while we validate. We can also enable a temporary SLA for the export route during the trial.
Thanks, Nadia + Redwood CS
```
Ethan Park: Reverted toggle and applied throttle. Monitoring shows duplicate rate dropped ~92%. Will keep sampling.
Riley Morgan: Dedupe script staged; ready for prod push after call. Preload script ready too.
Nadia Ahmed: Owners & deadlines:
- Jules: customer email + one-pager (owner) - due in 4h
- Ethan: toggles & throttles (owner) - done
- Riley: dedupe + preload + metrics (owner) - push after call
- Samir: commercial terms & downgrade matrix (owner) - draft in 6h
Call scheduled for 9am PT tomorrow. If no agreement within 48h post-call, escalate to @Head_CS.
Samir Desai: Draft uploaded to shared drive: https://drive.redwood/hf-save-1952. Added downgrade matrix and conversion incentives.
Jules Kim: One-pager and email sent to Nadia for review. Also included a short NPS + friction survey to run after trial.
Nadia Ahmed: Great work. If call successful, target conversion to a 3-month reserved uplift. If they instead choose downgrade, follow the staged downgrade to preserve revenue-critical routes. Tag updates here.

title: #customer-success: 1940129876
preview:
Maya Chen: FYI — I'm starting a proactive perf check for Larkspur (onboarding account). Seeing p95 ~740ms on gen calls and unit cost ~ $12 / 1M tokens. Renewal conversation in ~9 weeks, flagging as moderate risk if we don't show improvements.
Ethan Park: Can you paste the current model + runtime config?
Maya Chen: model=redwood/long-llm-7b @v2, quantization=none, region=us-west-2, batching=1, kv_cache=disabled, routing=default-tiered
Ravi Iyer: Quick read: we've seen this profile before. My recommendation: 1) swap to rq4g-v2 (4-bit quant) as a canary, 2) enable per-session KV prefix caching, 3) route lower-priority analytic calls to spot/dedicated-cheap pool in us-west-2, 4) do 10% canary -> 50% rollout with automatic rollback on quality delta >1.5%
Maya Chen: Ran a small sim on their prompt set — rq4g-v2 numbers look promising: p95 drops ~740ms -> ~420ms, median latency shaved ~35%, estimated token cost -28%. Note: we saw occasional tokenization variance on >1k tokens.
Jo Martinez: Customer ask from last call: they need transcripts to be near-deterministic for billing downstream. Any expected regressions there with 4-bit quant? We should capture that in the eval.
Ethan Park: I'll create a focused eval harness: golden prompt set (50 examples) + metrics: exact-match for critical fields, embedding drift, and semantic similarity. Will run baseline (v2) vs rq4g-v2 and post results here. ETA: 24h.
Ravi Iyer: Rollout snippet for ops (example):
```
routing:
  - rule: low_priority_to_spot
    match: request.tags.priority == 'low'
    target_pool: us-west-2-spot
model_override:
  base: redwood/long-llm-7b@v2
  canary: rq4g-v2
canary: 0.10
quality_monitor:
  delta_threshold: 0.015
  check_set: larkspur-golden-50
```
Maya Chen: I'll coordinate the enablement session with Larkspur — proposing Wed 11am PT. Jo can you own scheduling and invite SE? @Jo Martinez
Jo Martinez: Got it, I'll send invite and include a short agenda: what changed, expected perf delta, QA checklist, rollback plan.
Ethan Park: Uploaded initial eval run: baseline -> rq4g-v2 diff: EM drop 0.8%, embedding cosine avg 0.992, hallucination flags +0.2%. Attaching full CSV to workspace link: https://redwood.workspaces/s/teams/larkspur/evals/1940129876
Maya Chen: Nice. Given those deltas, ROI looks good. I'll draft the customer-facing note: perf changes, small QA caveat, suggested canary window of 2 days.
deploy-bot: Canary deployment started for project:larkspur canary_id:can-1940129876 at 1940131200
Ravi Iyer: Monitoring hooks: added trace for kv-hit-rate and model-variance. Alert if kv-hit < 30% or p95 increases above baseline.
Jo Martinez: After enablement we'll capture feedback items in the renewal playbook and route any feature asks to product.
Maya Chen: Action items: 1) Ethan — finalize eval & share detailed report (due +24h); 2) Jo — schedule enablement (due tomorrow); 3) Ravi — apply routing + monitoring canary (done); 4) Maya — send customer note post-canary. Assign and ack here.
Ethan Park: Ack.
Jo Martinez: Ack.
Ravi Iyer: Ack.
Maya Chen: :thumbsup:
deploy-bot: Canary health check passed at 1940132300 (p95=410ms, kv-hit=47%, quality-delta=0.9%)
Maya Chen: Thanks team — will update account health dashboard and flag improvement to Renewal lead.
Jo Martinez: I'll capture the customer's feedback during enablement and add to renewal risk tracker.
Maya Chen: Closing thread for now, will re-open if canary needs rollback or customer escalates.

title: #customer-success: 1918750001
preview:
Aisha Khan: FYI customer ACME just escalated — after the v3.2-quant rollout their invoice-parsing accuracy dropped ~30% for a specific prompt set. Internal evals (same test suite) show no regression. Can we triage? :eyes:
Ben Carter: assigning to eng-ml + runtime. Do we have the customer prompt and an example failure?
Aisha Khan: pasted below (sanitized). They say it used to return a structured JSON with fields, now sometimes returns free text or misses the 'invoice_total' field entirely. Customer prompt:
```
Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total
Invoice text: "ACME Corp\u00A01001
Date: 2025-11-05
Total: $4,320.00"
```
Note: the space between 'ACME Corp' and '1001' appears to be a non-breaking space (\u00A0) in the original customer payload.
Priya Nair: interesting — internal tests probably used normal spaces. Tokenization/normalization differences could explain mismatch. Did rollout change tokenizer or quantization that affects token boundaries?
Evan Li: runtime didn't change tokenizer intentionally. v3.2-quant included new fused attention kernel and a different packing path. Quantization can change decoding slightly. But 30% is big. Do we have the exact API input/response from the customer?
Aisha Khan: customer sent their raw request (sanitized):
```json
{"model":"redwood-v3-quant","input":"Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total
Invoice text: \"ACME Corp\u00A01001
Date: 2025-11-05
Total: $4,320.00\"","temperature":0.0}
```
Customer observed response:
```json
{"vendor":"ACME Corp1001","invoice_number":"","invoice_date":"2025-11-05","invoice_total":"4320.00"}
```
Expected (pre-rollout):
```json
{"vendor":"ACME Corp","invoice_number":"1001","invoice_date":"2025-11-05","invoice_total":"4320.00"}
```
support-bot: Created ticket #CS-4321 and linked ACME's logs.
Ben Carter: can we reproduce locally by sending the exact payload (with NBSP) to the canary and to previous model variant? Priya, can you try tokenizing that string in our tokenizer to see token boundaries?
Priya Nair: running tokenizer now. Quick result: when the NBSP is present, tokenizer produces a single token spanning "Corp\u00A01001" for the v3 tokenizer config. With normal space it splits into two tokens. That would explain vendor+invoice_number concatenation at generation with deterministic decoding.
Evan Li: good find. So internal evals likely normalized whitespace before tests. ACME's prompt preserved NBSP. Two paths: (1) update our normalization on ingest to normalize NBSPs, (2) add a check in rollout to run customer prompt sets unchanged. Which is safer short-term?
Aisha Khan: customer asks for quick mitigation — they prefer a patch on our side so they don't need to change their pipeline. Can we enable a preprocessing normalization for text inputs for hosted API only?
Ben Carter: engineering wise, adding a sanitizer that normalizes NBSP -> regular space before tokenization is low-risk. But need to audit for potential side effects (languages where NBSP matters). We can gate it behind a feature flag for the hosted API.
Priya Nair: I'll prototype a normalized path and run the customer's prompt set across canary vs prod. Also will re-run internal evals with NBSP variants to estimate regression sensitivity.
Evan Li: +1. Also check the quantized model's tokenization behavior doesn't differ across builds — upstream tokenizer weights/config mismatch is possible. Log the tokenizer config used in the model artifact.
Aisha Khan: communicating to customer: "we're investigating; temporary mitigation available (flag + canary) — we'll follow up in <4h with plan." They confirmed that's acceptable.
Ben Carter: action items: Priya prototype sanitizer + tests, Evan validate model tokenizer config and artifact consistency, QA run customer's prompt set with NBSP and normal space on v3.1 and v3.2-quant, Support update CS-4321 with ETA.
Priya Nair: started local repro: curl to canary shows the same concatenation. Example curl:
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"redwood-v3-quant","input":"Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total
Invoice text: \"ACME Corp\u00A01001
Date: 2025-11-05
Total: $4,320.00\"","temperature":0.0}'
```
Response reproduces customer case. Running tokenizer patch now.
Evan Li: if sanitizer fixes it, we should rollout to hosted quickly and then schedule a model-artifact audit for Dedicated/Private customers with a note about tokenizer parity.
Aisha Khan: customer wants notes for their compliance team. Can we include a short explanation of the root cause and patch plan in CS-4321?
Ben Carter: yes, include: root cause (NBSP tokenized as part of token -> concatenation), short term fix (input normalization on hosted API), medium term (artifact/tokenizer parity checks + eval harness include raw customer prompt sets), long term (add whitespace-varied tests in release gating).
Priya Nair: patch works in local canary — vendor and invoice_number separate now. I'll open PR and request urgent review.
support-bot: PR #8765 opened by priya.nair (label: urgent, changelog: input-normalization).
Evan Li: great. Once PR merges, let's run the customer's full prompt set, then canary deploy and notify ACME after verification.
Aisha Khan: thanks everyone. I'll keep CS-4321 updated and share reproduction steps with ACME for transparency. :handshake:

title: #customer-success: 1901234567
preview:
Maria Gomez: Quick weekly check-in for Quasar Health — they had 3 repeat 503s Friday afternoon (approx 15:02 UTC) during a billing spike. I logged the customer ticket (CS-8742). Can we confirm owners and next steps?

Ben Carter: I saw that. Root cause looks like connection pool exhaustion on the dedicated gateway. Error sample: `ERR_HTTP2_STREAM_CANCEL` in the logs.

Tim Liu: Assigned infra action. I'll take ownership of the pool tuning and short-term mitigations. Planning: bump pool size + add stricter circuit-breaker.

Aisha Patel: Thanks @Tim. I'll own the customer comms and update CS-8742 with the mitigation ETA. Also flagging renewal risk if this repeats.

Ben Carter: Short-term patch pushed to staging now. Deploy Bot: canary results?

Deploy Bot: Canary deploy to quasar-canary-2 completed at 2029-11-02T16:18:30Z — 0 errors in 20m smoke tests. :white_check_mark:

Tim Liu: Rolling the config to prod tonight at 22:00 UTC with a 10-minute canary. Action items: (1) increase pool from 200 -> 400, (2) add connection backoff on 5xx, (3) enable prefix KV cache for billing route.

Ben Carter: Adding a retry-after header handler on the client side to reduce retry storms. I'll open infra ticket INF-2198 and link to CS-8742.

Aisha Patel: I'll schedule a 15-min sync with the Quasar PM on Monday to walk through mitigations and offer an enablement doc for their engineers (best practices for retries + idempotency).

Maria Gomez: Ownership summary for the thread: Tim = infra/migration, Ben = client-side mitigations + INF-2198, Aisha = customer comms + enablement, Maria = escalation owner and renewal flag. Please confirm and drop any blockers.

Tim Liu: Confirmed. No blockers from infra, just need escalation window tonight (22:00-22:30 UTC).

Ben Carter: Confirmed. Will have INF-2198 link by EOD.

Aisha Patel: Confirmed. I'll send the calendar invite for Mon 10:00am PT and update CS-8742 with the interim workaround.

Maria Gomez: Excellent. Follow-ups: Aisha to post customer-facing notes, Tim to update post-deploy metrics in #eng-monitoring, Ben to attach client patch. We'll reconvene if repeat errors show up. :rocket:

Ava Chen: Pulling queries now, will drop raw numbers. Quick snippet: `SELECT date, active_users FROM usage WHERE account = 'greenmeadow' ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 60;` seeing a sharp drop starting ~2026-02-10. Workspace 'ops' impacted most.
Jordan Kim: Noted. My read —1) usage drop + procurement change = higher churn risk, 2) competitive eval means pricing/feature gaps in perception. Recommend immediate actions: schedule exec check-in (Diego), technical deep-dive to unblock (Ava), and a targeted enablement + ROI deck for new procurement (Jordan/Maya).

Ava Chen: No infra deploys in that window. Saw a config tweak in their integration (retry/backoff) that reduced call volume — I’ll capture exact change and a repro.

Maya Patel: Also worth offering a short remediation workshop (30-45m) to walk their platform team through caching and batching tips — offer 1 complimentary session to re-engage. If they mention price, offer a limited credit for a performance pilot.

Ava Chen: I’ll be there.

title: #customer-success: 1780003456
preview:
Alex Kim: quick sync — we need a short customer-facing RCA and timeline for ACME. They saw degraded embeddings for ~45m yesterday. Can we draft a safe summary and a follow-up plan? @Maria you own comms, @Devon please add what happened on call.
IncidentBot: Incident 2026-03-14-EMB-42 created by oncall_devon at 2026-03-14T02:40:12Z — severity: high. Paging: dev-oncall, eng-sre, customer-success.
Devon Patel: tl;dr — KV-cache corruption on a dedicated pool led to 5–25s tail latencies for embedding requests. Happened after a rolling kernel upgrade on the host fleet; one node failed to clear stale cache pointers so some inference workers served stale pointers -> OOM-ish behavior and long GC stalls. The autoscaler mitigated after 18m and we evicted the bad hosts manually.
Sophie Chen: adding details — kernel upgrade triggered a rare libnuma reorder. Repro locally with a stress runner and exact kernel + drm drivers. We rolled a hotfix that prevents the cache pointer reuse and forced a restart on affected hosts. Patch PR: https://git.internal/redwood/infra/commit/abc123
Devon Patel: timeline (local notes):
- 02:34 UTC: alert — embedding p95 > 2s, errors spike
- 02:36 UTC: oncall paged, started triage
- 02:45 UTC: identified hosts with repeated mem faults, isolated
- 02:58 UTC: autoscaler evicts/replicates; partial recovery starts
- 03:12 UTC: manual host drain and restart completed
- 03:19 UTC: monitoring shows latency back to baseline; closing incident
Maria Lopez: Draft customer message (short + non-technical):
"Hi ACME team — we want to follow up on the embedding degradation you experienced on 2026-03-14. Briefly: a software update on our host fleet caused a small subset of hosts to behave incorrectly, which slowed some embedding requests for about 45 minutes. We identified the issue, rolled a fix, and your workload recovered. There is no evidence of data loss. We will: 1) ensure the kernel update process includes this driver check, 2) add canary staging to that patch path, and 3) alert you if we see related anomalies. If you want a deeper technical RCA or a call, let us know and we can schedule. — Maria, Customer Success"
Ethan Park: ACME contact is OK with a short note — they asked for ETA on permanent mitigation and whether we can add a Dedicated health check for them.
Sophie Chen: Permanent mitigation plan (engineering phrasing for CS):
- Backport hotfix to kernel-update orchestration to skip nodes with the conflicting driver version.
- Add an automated preflight that runs a lightweight KV-cache integrity check after kernel change and before returning nodes to the pool.
- Add observable metric: kv_cache_pointer_reuse_count and an alert at >0 for 1m.
- Run a targeted canary (5% of capacity) for 24h on future host updates.
Devon Patel: Customer-impact assessment: Affected percentage of ACME requests ~3.8% during window; most requests were slightly delayed, <1% saw transient errors that retried successfully. No data exfiltration or model corruption.
Maria Lopez: Proposed status page update (short):
"We experienced a partial degradation of embedding latency for some customers on 2026-03-14 between 02:34–03:19 UTC due to a host-level software update. Services have recovered. Root cause: host kernel/driver interaction causing KV-cache instability. Remediation: host drains and a hotfix; longer-term mitigations in progress. For customers who saw impact, CS will follow up individually." — do we want to add the incident link?
Alex Kim: yes include the status link: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/emb-2026-03-14 — keep wording non-jargony.
Maria Lopez: OK publishing now. I'll send the short customer email and CC engineering. Draft reply to ACME included above. Anyone want to tweak wording?
Sophie Chen: One small tweak: change "host kernel/driver interaction" -> "host software update interacting with a driver version" (less blame-y).
Maria Lopez: updated. Final customer email queued. Action items: @Sophie to open a ticket for the preflight check (ENG-1429), @Devon to add the metric + alert (OBS-560), @Alex to confirm follow-up call if ACME requests.
Devon Patel: logging snippet from the incident for internal RCA:
```
[02:35:19] worker-7 ERROR kv_cache.cpp: pointer reuse detected, refcnt mismatch
[02:35:20] worker-7 WARN memory_manager.cpp: attempt to serve with stale pointer -> paused

```

preview:
Diego Morales: flag from renewal check-ins: three customers mentioned the same UX friction around model selection. feels like a cross-account signal. wondering if we should standardize how we surface multi-account recurring asks.
Leah Chen: agreed. PMs miss the pattern when items are scattered. what if CS created a tiny "Lighthouse Card" we can pin to the top of the notes so PMs see the pattern immediately?
Priya Singh: like a lighthouse that highlights repeated pain across customers? i like the metaphor. can you propose a compact template?
Diego Morales: 2-line template suggestion below 5 paste into notes or thread reply.
```
Lighthouse Card
- Issue (1 short sentence) + sample account count
- Business effect (renewal risk / onboarding delay / revenue impact)
- Suggested short-term workaround
- Priority: P0/P1/P2
- Tag(s): onboarding | renewal-risk | integrations | ux
```
Ben Carter: can we add a tiny field: "pattern evidence" (list of 1-2 lines with customer names + quote) so PMs can see it's cross-account without digging? keeps it honest.
Diego Morales: good call. updated sample with evidence line and an example filled card.
```
Lighthouse Card
- Issue: model selection defaults hide custom models (3 accounts reported)
- Business effect: blocks onboarding / renewal risk for two pilots (~$10k ARR risk)
- Workaround: enable "show all" toggle in UI for affected orgs
- Pattern evidence: AcmeRetail: "we can't find model X"; NovaHealth: "defaults hide our model"
- Priority: P1
- Tags: onboarding | renewal-risk | ux
```
Leah Chen: love the example. can ProductOps give us a shared sheet / export so cards roll into product triage automatically?
ProductOps Bot: Created shared template: https://go.redwood.ai/cs/lighthouse-cards (editable). Rows labeled "renewal-risk" auto-export to triage board on Fridays. :robot_face:
Priya Singh: pilot plan: 2-week pilot across onboarding + renewal outreach. target: 15 cards, at least 5 with cross-account evidence. any P0 -> immediate PM escalation.
Diego Morales: i'll add "create 1 Lighthouse Card" to the renewal call checklist for this week and try to pull evidence into the sheet.
Ben Carter: RenewalBot also flagged three accounts with similar NPS comments this morning. @RenewalBot can you attach the NPS snippets to the sheet?

Leah Chen: great. acceptance criteria for pilot: 1) 15 cards captured in 2 weeks, 2) at least 5 show cross-account pattern evidence, 3) PM triage receives export weekly and creates at least 1 follow-up ticket if priority >= P1.
Priya Singh: i'll run a short retro at the end of pilot and recommend next steps (embed card macro in Confluence / or add to onboarding playbook).
Diego Morales: thanks team. small structure, big signal. i'll post first cards later today from AcmeRetail call.
Leah Chen: excited to see if this reduces churn surprises. :sparkles:

title: #customer-success: 1769461199
preview:
Jasmine Liu: Kicking off a draft “Marketplace Customer Onboarding Playbook” for CS/Support. Goal: make it super clear what’s self-serve vs when we open a Support ticket vs when we pull in SE. Also need a short escalation matrix we can paste into internal handbook.

Rachel Kim: +1. We keep getting the same questions: entitlement delays, “I subscribed but can’t create org / no quota”, private offer activation timing, and billing dims mismatch. We should standardize the triage path.

Dev Patel: Yep. From Support side I want 2 things:
1) macros we can use (copy/paste)
2) explicit criteria when to route to SE vs keep in Support vs open Eng escalation.

Markus Klein: SE perspective: we’re happy to help when it’s blocking a big eval/POC, but a lot of these are pure entitlement plumbing. If we define “SE triggers” based on deal size / Dedicated/Private / tight timeline, it’ll save churn.

Alex Martinez: For docs, we can link the playbook to the marketplace onboarding guides once URLs are final. If we can keep the playbook structured around the customer journey (subscribe → entitlement → org provisioning → API key → first call), it’ll map cleanly to docs pages.

Jasmine Liu: Draft outline (internal):
A) Intake: confirm marketplace (AWS vs GCP), subscription ID, customer email domain, Redwood org (if exists)
B) Day 0 onboarding steps + common blockers
C) “When to open a Support ticket” (SUP) vs “SE engagement” vs “Security/GRC”
D) Escalation matrix + SLAs
E) Comms templates (customer-facing)

Ben Carter: For funnel instrumentation we need to be consistent on the “blocker categories” too. Even if it’s a Support ticket, tag it so we can track dropoffs.

Chloe Martin: FYI console onboarding errors we’re showing:
- `ENTITLEMENT_NOT_FOUND`
- `ENTITLEMENT_PENDING_PROPAGATION`
- `ORG_LINK_CONFLICT`
- `SUBSCRIPTION_ALREADY_LINKED`
If the playbook uses the same wording, it’ll reduce confusion.

Dev Patel: Can we agree on default waiting guidance? Right now reps are saying everything from “5 minutes” to “24 hours”.

Markus Klein: AWS is usually pretty fast, but propagation delays do happen. GCP has been slower in my experience. We should be careful not to promise a hard number.

Rachel Kim: Proposal:
- First-touch guidance: “Entitlement can take up to ~30–60 minutes to propagate. If you’re still blocked after 60 minutes, contact Support and include your Marketplace subscription ID.”
- Internally: if >60 min, open SUP ticket + check entitlement sync logs.

Chloe Martin: That aligns with what we’ve seen in dry runs. We also need to tell customers to hit “Retry entitlement check” (button) and not create a second org.

Jasmine Liu: Ok writing this down as policy:
1) If customer subscribed <60 min ago → Support responds with “pending propagation” macro + asks for subscription ID + suggests retry.
2) If >60 min and still `ENTITLEMENT_NOT_FOUND` → Support opens SUP ticket (queue: Marketplace Onboarding), assigns severity based on impact.
3) If customer is enterprise + time-bound launch → SE can parallel-path to keep momentum, but ticket still gets opened.

Dev Patel: Please define severity:
- Sev3: single customer blocked onboarding, no workaround
- Sev2: multiple customers or known incident (entitlement sync degraded)
- Sev1: widespread outage / sync job failing globally
Also: whenever Sev2+, page Eng on-call.

Rachel Kim: Add “workarounds” section:
- Confirm they used correct cloud account (AWS payer account vs linked account issues)
- Confirm region (they might be trying to provision in a region we don’t support for that SKU)
- If they already have a Redwood org, they must LINK not CREATE

Markus Klein: SE triggers (suggested):
- Dedicated or Private SKU interest (they bought Hosted but want Dedicated/Private evaluation)
- Private offer / committed spend negotiation in-flight
- Customer needs architecture review (VPC, KMS, audit logs) *and* has a security meeting scheduled
- Customer is >$XXK/yr forecast OR exec-sponsored
(We can fill the $ threshold after Jordan/Steph align.)

Jasmine Liu: Love it. Let’s also explicitly say: “SE is not required for basic Hosted API onboarding.” Otherwise we keep getting pulled in for password resets :)

Alex Martinez: Docs linkage ideas:
- “How marketplace onboarding works” page
- “Troubleshooting entitlements” page
- “Security for marketplace buyers” page
In playbook we can use short links + internal notes like “If docs are outdated, file a Docs issue + paste screenshot of console error.”

Ben Carter: Tagging: can we standardize ticket tags? e.g.
- `mktpl-entitlement-delay`
- `mktpl-org-linking`
- `mktpl-api-key`
- `mktpl-billing-dimension`
This helps metrics.

Dev Patel: Also billing dimension mismatch should *not* go to SE first. That’s Support → Billing/Eng. We’ve had cases where customer sees unexpected dimension name in AWS bill.

Rachel Kim: Yes. Add rule:
- Billing/reporting confusion → Support first.
- If it’s a contract interpretation / PO language → route to Partnerships/Finance (not SE).

Jasmine Liu: Ok, customer-facing macros (draft language):
1) Pending propagation: “Thanks — marketplace entitlements can take some time to propagate. Please click Retry in the Redwood Console. If you’re still blocked after ~60 minutes from subscription, reply with your subscription ID and we’ll investigate.”
2) Org linking conflict: “It looks like this subscription is already linked to a Redwood org. If you intended to use a different org, tell us the org slug and we can help verify the correct linkage.”
3) Private offer activation: “Private offers may take additional time to activate on the marketplace side. If you share the offer ID / subscription ID, we can confirm receipt and expected activation window.”

Chloe Martin: Add a line telling them where to find subscription ID (AWS console / GCP marketplace page). People keep sending screenshots of the wrong page.

Markus Klein: For enterprise onboarding, SE should provide a “first-call” validation + best practices (rate limits, streaming, retries). But again, only after entitlement is confirmed.

Dev Patel: Operational detail: Support should always ask for:
- Marketplace: AWS/GCP
- Subscription ID
- Redwood org slug (if any)
- Console error code
- Time of subscription (approx)
Then we can reproduce faster.

Jasmine Liu: Action items:
- Jasmine: turn this into a 1-pager playbook + escalation matrix by EOW
- Dev: draft macros in Support tool + propose tags
- Markus: propose SE trigger thresholds + owner list (rotation)
- Alex: confirm docs URLs + where playbook should live
- Chloe/Ben: confirm error code strings + instrumentation alignment

Rachel Kim: :thumbsup: Once there’s a draft, I’ll sanity check it against recent onboarding cases + add “do/don’t” notes for comms (avoid promising exact approval timelines).

title: #customer-success: 1767777770
preview:
Nina Alvarez: Quick note: deployed a lightweight 'field probe' run for Shoreline on Monday. Objective: smooth first-week UX, early renewal signals, and a 1-page handoff for AEs. Sharing ops notes here.
Ethan Park: Nice. Any quick checklist we can hand to the customer success manager?
Nina Alvarez: Yep. High-level: 1) Prefill: run 5k tokens spread over 12h within first 24h. 2) Cache: enable convo prefix cache with TTL=4h (set to 30m for trial/free tier). 3) Batch: batch_max_tokens=96, max_batch_size=8. 4) Streaming: stream=true for agent UI; client-side stream_timeout_ms=250 and client should show partial tokens. 5) Telemetry: record prompt_family, fallback_variant, latency_bucket, and session_n in CS telemetry.
Riya Singh: For renewal signals, add a simple health score: usage_ratio = current_week_tokens / expected_week_tokens. If usage_ratio < 0.5 for two consecutive weeks, flag for AE outreach.
Callum O'Neill: Also track NPS micro-pulse: after 3 successful sessions send inline 2-question pulse (satisfaction + blocker). Keep to in-app toast not modal.
Ethan Park: On streaming fallback: prefer auto-route to smaller variant rather than failing. Route chain: [shoreline-4x, shoreline-3q, shore-2s]. If fallback used >2% in first 48h, pause high-throughput prefill and notify eng.
Nina Alvarez: Agreed. Add fallback_threshold:2% and pause_prefill_on_exceed:true to the run metadata.
Riya Singh: Capturing enablement cadence: AE check-ins on day 2 and day 10, CS health pulse day 7, ops triage if any fallback spikes or >5 slow calls (>1s).
CS-Bot: JOB: field-probe-shoreline prefills completed. Summary: 98% success, 6 slow calls, 3 model fallbacks.
Callum O'Neill: Can someone post traces for the slow calls? Want to know if it's network vs model decode.
Ethan Park: I'll run a sampling query: `SELECT trace_id, duration_ms, stage FROM traces WHERE job='field-probe-shoreline' AND duration_ms>1000 ORDER BY duration_ms DESC LIMIT 10;` Posting results in 20m.
Nina Alvarez: Also, add a benign label prefill:intent=onboarding to job so SRE doesn't alert on expected spike.
Riya Singh: For renewal-risk automation: create ticket CS-7201 when usage_ratio drops AND pulse_satisfaction < 4. Auto-assign to AE + CS lead.
Callum O'Neill: For customer-facing one-pager, include: quick rollback (disable prefill job, set cache TTL=0), expected throughput, contact list (AE, CS, SRE Pager), and what to expect in the first 72h. Keep it printable.
Ethan Park: Noted. Also recommend instrumentation: add event 'session.prefill' with metadata {prompt_family, tokens_prefilled, fallback_used}.
Nina Alvarez: Action items: Ethan -> traces + event schema; Riya -> automation (CS-7201) + pulse copy; Callum -> one-pager; I'll coordinate AE handoff and close loop.
CS-Bot: REMINDER: create runbook entry under /cs/onboard/field-probes for Shoreline.
Ethan Park: Traces posted. Two slow calls show increased db latency on tenant side, one shows model queueing spike.
Riya Singh: Drafted pulse copy: "Quick check: Did the assistant feel helpful? Any blockers? (2 taps)" Short and optional. Posting to thread.
Callum O'Neill: One-pager draft uploaded to thread. Includes rollback steps, expected metrics, and AE email template.
Nina Alvarez: Perfect. Setting follow-up meeting for 15m after everyone reviews. Thanks team.
CS-Bot: NOTE: Thread will auto-archive after 7 days of inactivity.

title: #customer-success: 1767077070
preview:
Asha Rao: FYI - getting multiple reports that EU trial activations are stuck at the "initializing index" step. I exported an affected list (~68 tenants). Need a short customer note + credit policy.

Ben Carter: Infra update: indexing consumers fell behind after a brief downstream 502 storm and one JVM pause on an indexer node. We've capped retries and bumped consumer parallelism at 1767077400. Drain ETA ~35m.

Elena Ruiz: Draft customer copy (short):

Elena Ruiz: "Hi — we're sorry. Some trial accounts in EU may see delayed activation and partial search results while we clear an indexing backlog. Engineers have applied mitigations and we expect normal service within 1 hour.

Impacted trials will receive a $200 credit automatically. If you need expedited handling, book a 1:1: https://cal.redwood.ai/cs-officehours

We'll follow up with an RCA. — Redwood Support"

Asha Rao: Looks good. Standardize credit: $200 for trials <6 months. Manual backfills only for high-touch/renewal-risk accounts. I'll DM the AE list for >$12k ARR.

Ben Carter: Deploy link: https://deploys.redwood.ai/roll/retry-cap-778 — success @1767077605. Queue drain progress: 30% @10m, 72% @30m.

DeployBot: Roll/retry-cap-778 completed — 5 instances updated — 1767077605

Tom Hsu: Quick ops note: don't promise blanket manual backfills in the external message. Offer case-by-case and route to CS booking.

Asha Rao: Agreed. Elena — send first wave to affected trials (in-app + email). I'll prepare a short FAQ: how you'll know it's fixed, credits, and how to request 1:1.

Elena Ruiz: First wave sent to affected list. Subject: "Update — temporary activation delays for some EU trials". Included booking link and credit note.

CS Bot: Incident INC-4981 logged — status: mitigated — affected=68 — office hours link attached.

Ben Carter: Full drain at 1767079860. No data loss detected. We'll add GC tuning and a consumer-lag alert in the postmortem.

Asha Rao: Timeline/checklist: 1) customer email (sent) 2) status page update when drain=100% 3) RCA + tailored renewal-risk comms within 24h 4) credits auto-applied + quick feedback survey within 48h. I'll coordinate AE outreach for flagged accounts.

Elena Ruiz: I'll post engagement metrics and survey link here once credits are applied. :email:
